meta.title: Space Station Launch Button
meta.description: A button that triggers a buzzer when pressed, simulating a launch countdown.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding how to use a button to trigger an output device like a buzzer.
meta.code_hint: Use digitalRead on the button pin and tone() on the buzzer pin when the button is pressed.

meta.title: Space Station Launch Button
meta.description: A button that triggers a buzzer when pressed, simulating a launch countdown.
meta.learning_goal: Understanding how to use a button to trigger an output device like a buzzer.
meta.code_hint: Use digitalRead on the button pin and tone() on the buzzer pin when the button is pressed.

walkthrough.instruction: Place the button onto the breadboard across the center gap. Top legs in row 12, columns E and F. Bottom legs in row 14, columns E and F.
walkthrough.tip: The button bridges the two halves of the breadboard.

walkthrough.instruction: Place the buzzer with the long leg (+) in row 17, column E and the short leg (−) in row 20, column E.
walkthrough.tip: The long leg is positive — mark it with a tiny + on the buzzer.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D2 to the breadboard at row 12, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 14, column G to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a green wire from Arduino pin D8 to the breadboard at row 17, column A.
walkthrough.tip: This wire carries the signal between Arduino and your component.

walkthrough.instruction: Connect a black wire from the breadboard at row 20, column D to the Arduino's GND pin.
walkthrough.tip: Black wires carry ground — the return path for electricity.
